place: Phillipsburg town, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Phillipsburg’s name comes from an Indian chief named Philip or a Phillips family that settled there.
The town first organized as a township in 1851, then became an incorporated town in 1861 after Lopatcong split off.
The Roseberry House, an 18th‑century Georgian manor, is preserved by the Phillipsburg Area Historical Society and listed on the National Register.
Belvidere Cemetery, dating back to 1834, holds the graves of several Civil War figures and is featured in the town’s Victorian Days tours.
The Warren County Museum, now the home of the Historical and Genealogical Society, sits on Garret D. Street and showcases regional artifacts.
